name: left-to-right
description: Ordinary colour-row left-to-right/forfeit rules are unchanged from classic Qwixx; an auto chain co-mark forfeits every earlier index in the partner's row exactly as a direct mark would.

steps.note: red 3 - to the left of an already-marked cell, forfeited
red 7, to the right - legal, skipping index 4 (red 6) forfeits it forever
red[4] (red 6) is a chain space, but forfeiture applies to chain spaces exactly like any other cell - it was skipped and is gone

green[6] (green 6) is chained to yellow[6] (yellow 8). Marking it auto-crosses yellow[6], but yellow's maxMarkedIndex is already 8 - the auto co-mark still lands on yellow[6] (ignores the normal rule) even though 6 < 8 would ordinarily be illegal
yellow[6] is now already marked (via the auto co-mark) - marking it again directly is illegal, same as any already-marked cell
